# desec_token (Ephemeral Resource)

Creates a short-lived deSEC API token on open and, by default, deletes it on close. Use `keep_on_close = true` to retain the token after Terraform finishes.

## Example Usage

```terraform
ephemeral "desec_token" "example" {
  name               = "ci-deploy-token"
  perm_create_domain = false
  keep_on_close      = false
}

output "deploy_token" {
  value     = ephemeral.desec_token.example.token
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `allowed_subnets` (List of String) List of IP addresses or CIDR subnets that may authenticate using this token. Defaults to `["0.0.0.0/0", "::/0"]` (unrestricted).
- `auto_policy` (Boolean) When `true`, automatically creates a permissive scoping policy for each domain created with this token.
- `keep_on_close` (Boolean) When `true`, the token is not deleted when Terraform closes the ephemeral resource. Defaults to `false`.
- `max_age` (String) Maximum token lifetime as a duration string (e.g. `"365 00:00:00"`). Set to `null` for no age limit.
- `max_unused_period` (String) Maximum allowed period of disuse before the token is invalidated (e.g. `"30 00:00:00"`). Set to `null` for no limit.
- `name` (String) Human-readable token name for reference purposes. Maximum 178 characters.
- `perm_create_domain` (Boolean) Whether this token may create new domains.
- `perm_delete_domain` (Boolean) Whether this token may delete domains.
- `perm_manage_tokens` (Boolean) Whether this token may manage tokens (list, create, modify, delete).

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) Timestamp of token creation in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) The token's UUID.
- `is_valid` (Boolean) Whether the token is currently valid (not expired).
- `last_used` (String) Timestamp of when the token was last used, or `null` if it has never been used.
- `owner` (String) Email address of the deSEC account that owns this token.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) The token's secret value used to authenticate API requests.
